_meta.keyKnowledge: We learn more learning strategies including connecting to what we already know, asking ourselves questions, explaining things to ourselves and teaching peers.

_source.w: We learn more learning strategies including connecting to what we already know, asking ourselves questions, explaining things to ourselves and teaching peers.
y: The more learning strategies we have, the more effectively we can tackle new challenges.
Connecting new learning to what we already know makes it stick better.
Teaching others is one of the best ways to deepen our own understanding.
eg: We connect new information to something we already know to help us understand it.
We ask ourselves questions while reading — 'Do I understand this?' — to check our learning.
We explain a concept to ourselves in our own words to make sure we get it.
We teach a partner what we have learnt and see if they understand.
We use a strategy that worked before and adapt it for a new situation.
We reflect on which learning strategies work best for us and why.
